title: Podman sysvinit support for windows wsl
body:
/kind feature

Due Docker Subscription Service Agreement update, especially in context of Docker Desktop for Windows, many devs have a need to switch to Podman, some projects use extensively docker-compose.yml. Unfortunatly **podman.socket**, as well as docker daemon, can not be used in WSL system because of discrepancy with **systemd**. But SysVinit works and it would be great if podman service socket could be run with it. It would make much easier to switch for a while and then migrate docker-compose.yml to pods and kubernetes yaml files.

WIndows 10 WSL v.2 Ubuntu- 20.04
Podman 3.3.1

systemd
```
serhii@wd:/usr/lib/systemd/user$ sudo systemctl start podman.socket
System has not been booted with systemd as init system (PID 1). Can't operate.
Failed to connect to bus: Host is down
```

sysvinit
```
serhii@wd:/usr/lib/systemd/user$ sudo service podman.socket start
podman.socket: unrecognized service
```

comments.body: You can manually run the service (`podman system service -t 0`) if you want to. Wrapping that invocation with a sysvinit service should be relatively simple? If someone wants to do it and PR it against the repo we'd be happy to merge it.

> You can manually run the service (`podman system service -t 0`) if you want to. Wrapping that invocation with a sysvinit service should be relatively simple? If someone wants to do it and PR it against the repo we'd be happy to merge it.

Thank you Matthew! Manual approach worked out for me smoothly
@SerhiiTopor is there anything more left to do in Podman or can we close the issue?
@giuseppe yep you can close it, thank you)
